Empires & Puzzles General Yin Review: The Tiger General Who Clears the Board

General Yin is a rarity among rares: a hero who dispels buffs BEFORE hitting. But does his Slow mana speed make him a tactical genius or a sitting duck? We analyze this tiger general's place in the 3-star tournament meta.
General Yin: The Slow But Sure 3-Star Dispeller
General Yin, The Tiger Tactician: Priority Dispel for the Masses
General Yin is a 3-star Rare Nature hero from the Journey family, released in early 2023. As an anthropomorphic tiger wielding a giant axe, he brings a unique utility to the 3-star arena:
. This means he removes enemy buffs
dealing damage, a mechanic usually reserved for higher-rarity heroes. However, this power comes at a cost:

General Yin boasts some of the most detailed artwork for a 3-star hero. He is a fierce, muscular tiger in ornate general's armor, evoking the style of classic Chinese literature. The "Enchanted Axe" he wields is massive and glowing, hinting at the magical nature of his strikes. Compared to older 3-star designs (looking at you, Friar Tuck), General Yin looks like he belongs in a 5-star lineup.
In the modern 3-star landscape (late 2025), General Yin's stats are solid but not game-breaking.
Respectable for a 3-star, allowing his hit-all special to deal meaningful damage.
These stats are average. He isn't a tank, but he isn't made of glass either. He needs these stats to survive long enough to charge his slow mana.
Standard for his release era, trailing behind the newest Toon costumes or Construct 3-stars.
"Enchanted Axe Swing" is a tactical nuke.

Deals +8% damage per removed status effect (up to 276% total). This is his punish mechanic. If the enemy team is fully buffed (e.g., Faiez, Gunner, Kai), General Yin hits them significantly harder.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

. He only deals damage and dispels. This is a confusing design choice that effectively leaves him without a functional family bonus unless paired with other Journey heroes for the stat boost (Morale Boost) in War of Three Kingdoms.

Upon release, he was compared favorably to Joukahainen (Blue dispeller) but criticized for his Slow speed. He found an immediate niche in 3-star Rush Tournaments.
The mechanic of "Dispel First" remains premium. While newer 3-stars hit harder or have faster mana, few offer this specific utility. He remains unique in Green.

is generally preferred for 3-star play. In Rush tournaments (his main home), he charges fast enough that survivability is less of an issue than impact. You want him to wipe the board after dispelling.
Removes defensive buffs before dealing damage. Best counter to Gunner, Faiez (def up), and counter-attackers.

The more the enemy buffs, the harder he hits.

Paradoxically good. Enemies rely on buffs; he removes them and hits harder because of them.

Keep him! A 3-star dispeller is rare and valuable. Even if he is slow, he solves problems that other 3-stars can't (like dealing with a fully buffed enemy team).
Limit Break him for your Rush Tournament depth. He is a green option for when Blue (Joukahainen) or Red (Tyrum) aren't allowed or aren't ideal.

Players immediately drew parallels to the Kalevala hero, debating who was better. The consensus: Joukahainen is faster (Average) and blocks buffs, making him generally superior, but Yin hits harder in the right conditions.
Frequent complaints about his family bonus being useless for him. "It's like Carol all over again!"
General Yin is a tool hero. You don't bring him for his DPS; you bring him because the enemy has Gunner, Kai, or Faiez, and you need those buffs
right now. His damage is just the icing on the cake. In the niche of 3-star Rush, he is a general worthy of his rank.
A valuable bench warmer who becomes MVP for one week every month (Rush Tournament).
The tiger doesn't just roar; he strips you of your defenses.
